regimeSub: Markets digest June CPI beat as BOK rate hike looms Thursday; risk-on fading into event risk.
themes: June CPI 3.5% vs 3.8% expected now priced → SPX consolidating at 7543 as July FOMC hike odds collapse, Warsh's hawkish rhetoric neutralized by data
BOK poised to hike to 2.75% Thursday per Korea Times → USDKRW stuck at 1490 as won weakness persists despite tightening cycle expectations
SK Hynix ADR/Seoul divergence (-15% Seoul crash vs +13% ADR) signals HBM earnings ceiling → KOSPI at 7251, down 20% from June 22 ATH of 9114
Brent at $85.93 as OPEC+ August quota expansion meets renewed Middle East risk → oil stabilizing at pre-Iran-war levels despite Trump ceasefire collapse

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: 10Y real ~1.12% (4.62 nominal - 3.5% CPI); compressed from peak as disinflation trade gains traction

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: 36bps steep (4.62 - 4.26); bear steepening intact as Fed hike odds fade but fiscal premium persists

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: VIX at 16.5 suggests spreads tight; credit complacent on soft landing narrative post-CPI

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: 16.5 subdued despite BOK decision and geopolitical risk; vol sellers in control post-CPI relief

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: $85.93 range-bound as OPEC+ quota hike offsets renewed Hormuz risk from Trump ceasefire exit

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: $4040 holding highs despite June's worst month since 2008; real rate compression supportive

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: 1490 sticky; BOK hike expectations not enough to reverse won weakness amid chip sector turmoil

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: SK Hynix Seoul -15% on HBM contract caps; contagion risk to SOXQ after 181% YTD rally

headline: CPI-driven relief rally stalls as markets await BOK Thursday hike and digest SK Hynix HBM collapse, with Warsh's July hike case materially weakened by 3.5% inflation print.
